# Synthesis Update — Added verilog/rtl Files

## Change Summary

Per user request to "synthesize everything under verilog/rtl", the `VERILOG_FILES` 
list in `openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/config.json` was updated.

## Files in verilog/rtl/

| File | Action | Reason |
|------|--------|--------|
| `mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.v` | Already included | Top-level wrapper (synthesis target) |
| `mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.v` | Already included | Compute subsystem |
| `mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_chip.v` | **Added** | Chip-level structural RTL |
| `padframe.v` | Excluded | Yosys parse error: `.DM[0]()` array port syntax unsupported |
| `core.v` | Excluded | Duplicate module: defines same `mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper` module |

## Result

- New total VERILOG_FILES: 82 (was 81)
- `mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_chip.v` added (defines chip top connecting padframe+wrapper)
- Synthesis completed: 0 errors
- New netlist: 650,477 lines
- Full PnR re-run started from Yosys.Synthesis

## Why padframe.v was excluded

`padframe.v` uses non-standard Verilog syntax for IO pad bus connections:
```
.DM[0](clk_dm_0),  // Bit-select in port connection — not valid Verilog-2005
.DM[1](clk_dm_1),
.DM[2](clk_dm_2),
```
This causes a Yosys parser error. The padframe is a hard-IP block (sky130 IO cells)
and is not synthesized — it's provided as a pre-hardened GDS.

## Why core.v was excluded

`core.v` (auto-generated padframe template) defines:
```verilog
